title: MiTAC Computex 2026 Booth Tour: Diamond Cooling, 52U Racks, and More
gist: At Computex 2026 MiTAC showed turnkey liquid-cooled AI and HPC racks built on AMD Instinct MI355X and EPYC silicon, Broadcom Tomahawk 5 networking, and Nidec CDUs, with a 52U rack packing 96 MI355X accelerators that the vendor claims delivers 50% more density than standard air-cooled AI configs.

insights.claim: Surging AI hardware demand has driven especially strong revenue for server vendors worldwide over the last couple of years, with new processors, shifting bottlenecks, and the recent explosion in agentic systems reshaping the server hardware needed.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Confirms an extended up-cycle for server OEMs/ODMs and signals that agentic workloads are a fresh demand driver reshaping system design, supporting continued capex exposure to the segment.
insights.quote: the recent explosion in agentic systems have been reshaping the kinds of server hardware needed

insights.claim: MiTAC is now one of Taiwan's largest server vendors, scale built partly via the AI boom and by folding in its Tyan brand and Intel's server business.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: MiTAC's consolidation of Tyan and Intel's server business makes it a larger AI-server consolidator worth tracking alongside the better-known Taiwanese ODMs.
insights.quote: MiTAC is one of Taiwan’s largest server vendors, thanks in part to the AI boom, but also with folding in its Tyan brand, and Intel’s server business

insights.claim: MiTAC's 52U liquid-cooled rack packs 12 G4826Z5 AI servers, each with 8 AMD Instinct MI355X accelerators, for 96 MI355X accelerators in a single rack, sold as a turnkey product.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Concrete rack-scale AMD Instinct deployment (96 GPUs/rack) is direct evidence of AMD MI355X commercialization at rack scale, a competitive datapoint versus Nvidia rack systems.
insights.quote: a grand total of 96 MI355X accelerators in a single rack
insights.locator: Big Iron: AMD Instinct MI355X 52U Liquid Cooled Server Rack

insights.claim: The MI355X rack uses Broadcom Tomahawk 5 800Gbps Ethernet switches for the networking backbone and a Nidec CDU capable of moving 200kW of heat.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Names the supply chain underneath AMD AI racks: Broadcom Tomahawk 5 for 800G Ethernet fabric and Nidec for high-capacity CDUs, both picks-and-shovels beneficiaries of liquid-cooled AI buildouts.
insights.quote: Broadcom is supplying the backbone of the system with its Tomahawk 5 800Gbps Ethernet switches

insights.claim: MiTAC promotes the 52U liquid-cooled rack as offering 50% more density per rack than standard AI configurations, which the article interprets as air-cooled systems.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Quantifies the density advantage of liquid cooling plus taller racks (~50%), reinforcing the structural shift away from air cooling in AI data centers.
insights.quote: offering 50% more density per rack than “standard” AI configurations
insights.locator: 'According to MiTAC, the super-sized server is designed specifically to maximize density'

insights.claim: Each 4U G4826Z5 server pairs two AMD EPYC 9xx5 processors with the MI355X GPUs and eight front U.2 drive bays, splitting GPUs in the top half and CPUs below.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Shows AMD winning both the GPU and host-CPU sockets in the same AI node, an all-AMD configuration that strengthens EPYC attach to Instinct deployments.
insights.quote: The 4U server rack contains two AMD EPYC 9xx5 processors alongside the aforementioned MI355X GPUs.

insights.claim: MiTAC's 48U OCP ORv3-compliant HPC rack uses C2811Z5 2U AMD EPYC 9555 compute servers (up to 3TB memory per node) paired with LE2S01 'Lake Erie' 2U SAS4 storage servers holding 36 3.5-inch drive bays.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Demonstrates AMD EPYC 9555 winning CPU-focused HPC racks built to OCP standards, evidence of EPYC strength beyond AI-accelerator nodes.
insights.quote: MiTAC installed its C2811Z5 2U AMD EPYC 9555 servers, which can accommodate up to 3TB of memory per node

insights.claim: The HPC rack's power and cooling are sourced externally, using a Murata power shelf and a 250kW Nidec CDU rather than MiTAC's own parts.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Murata (power shelves) and Nidec (250kW CDUs) recur as critical third-party suppliers into liquid-cooled racks, useful for mapping the cooling/power supply chain.
insights.quote: they had a Murata power shelf, as well as a sizable 250kW CDU from Nidec
insights.locator: 'Meanwhile, no rack would be complete without power...'

insights.claim: Taiwanese server vendors at Computex 2026 are demonstrating diamond cooling for AI GPUs as a next-step thermal capability.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Diamond cooling emerging on the show floor flags the next frontier in GPU thermal management as power densities climb, a niche materials/cooling theme to watch.
insights.quote: neat capabilities like diamond cooling AI GPUs
